name: Bélmez from Peñarroya-Pueblonuevo — circular by the Vía Verde del Guadiato and Los Pedroches
name_original: Bélmez desde Peñarroya-Pueblonuevo — circular por la Vía Verde del Guadiato y Los Pedroches
region_intro: Hiking around Peñarroya-Pueblonuevo offers diverse landscapes within the Sierra Morena region, characterized by a mix of natural beauty and historical significance. The terrain includes extensive Mediterranean forests of chaparros, cork oaks, and holm oaks, alongside pastures and mountains that define the Guadiato Valley. Hikers will encounter varied vistas, from urban and agricultural areas to remnants of the region's mining past, and scenic reservoirs like Embalse de Sierra Boyera. Best hiking trails around Peñarroya-Pueblonuevo The most popular hiking route is Bélmez from Peñarroya-Pueblonuevo — circular by the Vía Verde del Guadiato and Los Pedroches, a 11.2 miles (18.0 km) trail that takes 4 hours 44 minutes to complete. This moderate route follows an old mining railway, offering varied scenery. Another top favourite among local hikers is Belmez and Sierra Boyera Reservoir Loop, a moderate 6.3 miles (10.2 km) path. This circular trail provides scenic views of the Embalse de Sierra Boyera and includes a walk across the dam. Local hikers also love the Belmez Castle loop from Belmez, a 4.4 miles (7.1 km) trail leading through historical areas, often completed in about 1 hour 54 minutes. Hiking around Peñarroya-Pueblonuevo is defined by Mediterranean forests, scenic reservoirs, and paths reflecting the region's mining heritage. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy walks to moderate trails. The routes in Peñarroya-Pueblonuevo are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.0 stars from 3 reviews. More than 20 hikers have used komoot to explore Peñarroya-Pueblonuevo's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in Peñarroya-Pueblonuevo?
A: There are 7 hiking trails documented in the Peñarroya-Pueblonuevo area. These routes offer a mix of easy and moderate options, catering to various fitness levels.

Q: What kind of landscapes can I expect to see while hiking in Peñarroya-Pueblonuevo?
A: The region offers diverse landscapes, from extensive Mediterranean forests of chaparros, cork oaks, and holm oaks to pastures and mountains of the Guadiato Valley. You'll also encounter urban and agricultural vistas, remnants of the area's mining past, and scenic reservoirs like the Embalse de Sierra Boyera.

Q: Are there any easy hiking routes suitable for beginners or families?
A: Yes, there are 3 easy hiking routes available. One option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20121979" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Belmez Castle loop from Belmez&lt;/a&gt;, which is 4.4 miles (7.1 km) long and takes about 1 hour 54 minutes to complete, leading through historical areas.

Q: Can I find circular hiking routes in Peñarroya-Pueblonuevo?
A: Yes, several routes are circular. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1249053303/belmez-and-sierra-boyera-reservoir-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Belmez and Sierra Boyera Reservoir Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 6.3-mile (10.2 km) path that offers scenic views of the Embalse de Sierra Boyera and includes a walk across the dam.

Q: What historical sites or landmarks can I explore on a hike?
A: The region has a rich history, particularly related to mining and the Spanish Civil War. You can explore sites like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5360266" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Belmez Castle&lt;/a&gt;, or walk along the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5593129" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Greenway of Mining&lt;/a&gt;, which follows an old mining railway and passes by historical wells. Some trails also feature vestiges of military constructions.

Q: Are there trails that offer views of reservoirs or water bodies?
A: Absolutely. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1249053303/belmez-and-sierra-boyera-reservoir-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Belmez and Sierra Boyera Reservoir Loop&lt;/a&gt; provides excellent views of the Embalse de Sierra Boyera, allowing you to walk across its dam. The presence of rivers like the Guadalora also contributes to picturesque trails.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Peñarroya-Pueblonuevo?
A: The routes in Peñarroya-Pueblonuevo are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.0 stars from 3 reviews. Hikers often praise the varied terrain, from Mediterranean forests to historical mining landscapes, and the well-documented nature of many paths.

Q: Are there any long-distance trails or greenways in the area?
A: Yes, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1248986408/belmez-from-penarroya-pueblonuevo-circular-by-the-via-verde-del-guadiato-and-los-pedroches" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bélmez from Peñarroya-Pueblonuevo — circular by the Vía Verde del Guadiato and Los Pedroches&lt;/a&gt; is a notable route. This 11.2-mile (18.0 km) trail follows an old mining railway, offering varied scenery and a comfortable path suitable for longer walks.

Q: What kind of wildlife can I spot while hiking?
A: The diverse ecosystems of the Sierra Morena, which includes this region, support a variety of birdlife. Hikers may observe cranes, kestrels, storks, Bonelli's eagles, and vultures during their walks.

Q: Are the hiking trails in Peñarroya-Pueblonuevo generally well-marked?
A: Based on local information, many hiking trails in and around Peñarroya-Pueblonuevo are well-documented and potentially signposted, making navigation easier for hikers.

Q: What is the general difficulty level of hikes in this region?
A: The hikes around Peñarroya-Pueblonuevo are primarily categorized as easy to moderate. There are 3 easy routes and 4 moderate routes, making the area accessible for a wide range of hikers.
tips: Once upon a time, there was a dog here known as "El Terrible" because of its ferocity. That dog is also credited with finding the first coal deposits in the area, around which a town arose, on the outskirts of Peñarroya, which was called Pueblonuevo El Terrible in honor of the feared dog to which they owed so much. Decades later, Peñarroya and Pueblonuevo joined forces, losing the dog's name along the way, but not forgetting him as they built a statue in his honor.

Belmez Castle is a historic fortress located in Belmez. It stands majestically on a high limestone rock, offering a stunning view of the village and surrounding areas.
The castle dates back to at least 1245, with significant additions made in the 15th century. It played an important role during the Reconquista and was occupied by French troops during the Peninsular War (1810-1812). The castle features a pentagonal Keep, six semicircular towers and a zigzag staircase leading up to it.
If you're planning a visit, it's open daily and admission is free.
A Vía Verde trail, going through the industrial heritage of Fuente del Arco-Peñarroya-Puertollano where an old railway line was transformed into a trail for adventure and outdoor activities.

waypoints.name: Statue of El Terrible Dog, Peñarroya-Pueblonuevo
